libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Do not rely on installing or asserting behavior of the deprecated SecurityManager on newer JDKs. Prefer normal OGNL coverage over user-defined POJOs, maps, lists, method calls, constructors, static fields/methods, and type conversion. If deliberately testing deep reflective access to JDK internals, upstream OGNL's JDK17+ Maven profile uses --add-opens for java.base/java.lang and java.base/java.util, but this is not required for ordinary meaningful coverage.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #7430 [Automation] java run fails for ognl:ognl:3.3.5; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Existing 3.2.21 tests include SecurityManager scenarios and a java.base/java.lang add-opens JVM arg; those legacy tests may be brittle on JDK 25 even though normal OGNL usage does not require extra setup.

OGNL initializes an expression compiler backed by Javassist; this is already declared as a normal transitive dependency of ognl:ognl:3.3.5, so adding it explicitly would be redundant unless Gradle resolution is later changed to exclude transitives.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: OGNL is an in-process expression language for navigating and mutating Java object graphs; its only runtime dependency for 3.3.5 is transitive Javassist, and meaningful tests can exercise normal Ognl.parseExpression/getValue/setValue usage against local POJOs without extra services, extra Maven dependencies, or required environment setup.
